title: A Polish Electroencephalography, Alzheimer’s Risk-genes, Lifestyle and Neuroimaging (PEARL-Neuro) Database
description: The PEARL-Neuro Database (version 1.1.0) comprises multimodal neuroimaging and genetic data from 192 healthy middle-aged individuals (50-63 years) at risk for dementia, with a neuroimaging subgroup of 79 participants. The dataset includes resting-state and task-based electroencephalography (EEG) and functional magnetic resonance imaging (fMRI), genetic polymorphisms (APOE and PICALM), psychometric assessments, and clinical blood biomarkers. This version includes marker corrections in EEG resting-state files (.tsv and .vmrk) to ensure consistency with the published Data Note, addressing missing starting markers for eyes-open and eyes-closed conditions in the resting-state protocol for 21 participants.
methods_description: EEG and fMRI data were acquired during resting-state protocols (eyes open and eyes closed) and cognitive tasks (multi-source interference task and Sternberg's memory task). Genetic analysis included APOE and PICALM polymorphisms. Psychometric assessments evaluated memory, intelligence, mood, personality, and stress coping strategies. Blood tests included complete blood count, lipid profile, and HSV serology.
